name: Bottom navigation
problem: Mobile users need fast access to a small set of top-level destinations.
solution: Place persistent primary destinations at the bottom edge so users can switch sections with one tap.
problemContext: A compact mobile interface has a small number of high-frequency primary sections.
Users need to switch between those sections repeatedly.
selectionRules: Choose bottom navigation for three to five top-level mobile destinations.
Prefer a navigation drawer or menu when there are many destinations or secondary areas.
Do not use bottom navigation for one-off actions that are not destinations.
requiredStates: Default state with all destinations visible.
Active destination state that is perceivable without relying on color alone.
Destination changed state with updated content and preserved orientation.
Unavailable destination state if permissions or feature flags hide access.
interactionContract: Each item changes the active top-level destination rather than triggering a transient action.
Labels or accessible names identify every destination.
The active destination remains visible while users navigate within that section.
implementationChecklist: Limit destinations to the primary sections users need most often.
Give every icon a visible or accessible label.
Keep destructive or contextual actions out of the navigation bar.
commonMisuses: Putting too many destinations into the bar.
Mixing navigation destinations with commands such as create or delete.
Using icon-only labels for unfamiliar sections.
critiqueQuestions: Are these items destinations the user returns to often, or just actions competing for attention?
useWhen: The product has a small number of high-frequency top-level destinations.
Users need frequent switching between major sections.
avoidWhen: There are too many primary destinations.
The destinations are low-frequency utilities or deep hierarchy items.
variants: Adaptive bottom nav to navigation rail
accessibility: The active destination must not be conveyed by color alone.
keyboardBehavior: External keyboard focus should move through destinations predictably.
Activation should change views without losing orientation.
failureModes: Using bottom navigation for unrelated actions.
Combining it with another competing primary navigation model.

sources.note: Android navigation guidance defines bottom navigation as a primary pattern for compact layouts.
